name: Beef Wellington (Gourmet Mag)
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 lb Beef fillet, tied and larded at room temperature
1/2 lb Pate de foie gras, at room temperature
1 large Egg yolk beaten with 1 tsp Water
2 tsp Arrowroot, dissolved in 1 tbsp Cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Roast beef in a preheated 400°F oven for 25-30 minutes or until a meat thermometer registers 120°F. Let cool completely and discard larding fat and strings. Skim pan juices and reserve.
3. In a heavy skillet, cook mushrooms in butter over medium-low heat, stirring, until all liquid is evaporated and mixture is dry. Season with salt and pepper and let cool.
4. Spread fillet evenly with foie gras, covering top and sides. Spread cooled mushrooms evenly over foie gras.
5. Roll 1 lb puff pastry into a rectangle about 20x12 inches on a floured surface. Invert fillet in the middle of the pastry and fold long sides to enclose it, brushing edges with egg white to seal.
6. Fold ends of dough over fillet and seal with egg white. Transfer seam side down to a jelly-roll pan and brush with egg wash. Roll out additional dough and cut shapes for decoration. Brush with egg wash.
7. Chill at least 1 hour and up to 2 hours.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F again. Bake fillet in the middle of the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
9. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake an additional 5-10 minutes or until a meat thermometer registers 130°F for medium-rare and pastry is cooked through. Let stand 15 minutes.
10. In a saucepan, reduce reserved pan juices and Madeira by one-fourth. Add arrowroot mixture, broth, truffles, salt, and pepper. Cook over moderate heat, stirring, for 5 minutes or until thickened without boiling.
11. Loosen fillet from the pan and transfer to a heated platter. Garnish with watercress. Serve fillet cut into 3/4-inch thick slices with sauce.
